meta.title: Hack Club Security Bounty Program
meta.description: Find vulnerabilities across Hack Club programs, disclose them responsibly, and earn bounties.

ui.submitReportNewTab: Submit a security vulnerability report (opens in a new tab)

hero.title: Hack Club Security Bounty Program
hero.tagline: Find security vulnerabilities & get cold, hard cash!

programs.label: Programs
programs.middle: is covered, along with any miscellaneous Hack Club HQ program. HCB run their own security program and you can find their policy

rewards.label: Payouts
rewards.desc: Note: These amounts are approximations and final bounty amounts are subject to our discretion. This list is subject to change.

rows.title: Medium severity
rows.desc: starting at $50; private email on Hack Club platforms, legal names, exact birthdate

rows.title: Root or admin
rows.desc: root on *.selfhosted.hackclub.com or hcb.hackclub.com, or admin on the vercel.com/@hackclub GitHub, outside Docker

rows.title: Non-root
rows.desc: access to any of the above, or RCE in a sensitive Docker container on our infrastructure. payout can go up depending on PII impact!

rows.title: Information disclosure or integrity violations
rows.desc: exposed admin panels, configuration issues, irrecoverably high impact destructive actions

rows.title: XSS, CSRF, Open Proxy/Redirect
rows.desc: paid according to interaction level & PII impact

payments.label: Payment methods

methods.name: WISE
methods.caption: International except for India, Iceland and Uganda

payments.note: Whichever program the bug is found in covers the payout.

scope.label: Out of scope
scope.desc: Any program not participating is out of scope. Generally also out of scope, not exhaustive:
items: scraping public Slack information or account enumeration
server side request forgery without proven meaningful impact
automated scanner output without real-world impact
denial of service through resource exhaustion
platform issues like on Slack that are outside our control

safeharbor.label: Safe harbor
body: We support good-faith security research: accessing a system only to find, investigate, or fix a security flaw, in a way that avoids harm to people or the public, and using what you learn to improve security.

For good-faith research conducted while this program is active, we will not bring or support legal action against you, including for bypassing measures that protect in-scope applications. If someone else brings legal action against you for that research, we will take steps to make known that you acted in good faith.

We ask the same care in return: respect privacy and make a good-faith effort not to access or destroy other people's data, report what you find promptly, and never exploit a vulnerability beyond what is needed to demonstrate it.

ai.label: AI reports
ai.body: Using AI to improve your report is fine, but submissions that are entirely AI-generated with no original input will not be accepted. We want original research with real-world impact. In some cases, a 0.5x or worse multiplier will be levied on valid reports that are hard to process! You may also be banned from participating in the program.

report.label: Reporting
report.body: Submit vulnerabilities through the form and we will follow up over email.
